title: The potential effects of meditation on age-related cognitive decline: a systematic review
journal: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
headline: The potential effects of meditation on age-related cognitive decline: a systematic review
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of mindfulness / meditation examined focus & attention. Growing evidence suggests that cognitive training programs may have the potential to counteract this decline. We conclude that meditation interventions for older adults are feasible, and preliminary evidence suggests that meditation can offset age-related cognitive decline.
keyFindings: Growing evidence suggests that cognitive training programs may have the potential to counteract this decline.
We conclude that meditation interventions for older adults are feasible, and preliminary evidence suggests that meditation can offset age-related cognitive decline.
limitations: However, most studies had a high risk of bias and small sample sizes.
Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
